metadata.title: Global Crop Land Cover Suitability Index — Enriched Agricultural Knowledge Base
metadata.source: FAO Agro-Ecological Zones (AEZ), ICAR Crop Requirement Guidelines, CGIAR Suitability Maps, NHRDF, APEDA, CropScience journals
metadata.description: Complete agricultural knowledge base for 100 crops with suitability percentages, growing conditions, fertilizer recommendations, best regions, and detailed explanations for each crop recommendation.

land_cover_classes.barren: Bare soil, arid/semi-arid land for hardy drought-tolerant varieties
land_cover_classes.rangeland: Grassland, savanna, shrubland for rainfed cultivation
land_cover_classes.water: Wetlands, shallow water bodies, irrigated paddy/aquatic systems

fields_per_crop.suitability_%: Favorable percentage of each land cover class for the crop
fields_per_crop.fertilizers: Recommended NPK ratios and organic amendments
fields_per_crop.explanation: Detailed reasoning for why the crop suits specific land cover conditions
fields_per_crop.key_practices: Essential cultivation, pest management, and harvest practices

crops.name: Rice (Paddy)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Clayey, alluvial, loamy with good water retention

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:40 kg/ha; zinc sulphate 25 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; green manuring with Sesbania
crops.best_regions_india: West Bengal, Punjab, UP, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Bihar
crops.explanation: Rice thrives in waterlogged paddy fields — high water land cover strongly favors it. Needs standing water for 80% of growth cycle. Alluvial soils of river deltas provide ideal nutrition. Not suitable for barren/arid or heavily urbanized zones.
crops.key_practices: Transplanting in puddled fields; maintain 5cm standing water; apply N in 3 splits (basal, tillering, panicle); integrated pest management for stem borer and BPH.

crops.name: Wheat

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained loamy, clay-loam, alluvial soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 150:60:40 kg/ha; DAP at sowing; urea top-dress at crown root & heading; ZnSO₄ 25 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Punjab, Haryana, UP, MP, Rajasthan, Bihar, Gujarat
crops.explanation: Wheat needs cool dry winters with moderate moisture — ideal in existing agricultural land with irrigation. Can tolerate semi-arid rangeland with supplemental irrigation. Poor in forest/water-logged areas.
crops.key_practices: Seed drill sowing at 20cm row spacing; 5–6 irrigations at CRI, tillering, jointing, flowering, dough stages; zero-till technology saves 20% water.

crops.name: Maize / Corn

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep fertile loamy, sandy-loam with good drainage

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:40 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; zinc 25 kg/ha; nitrogen in 3 splits
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka, Rajasthan, MP, Bihar, UP, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana
crops.explanation: Maize is versatile — grows on cropland and cleared rangeland. Tolerates moderate slopes (forest edges). Needs well-drained soil (fails in waterlogged). High nitrogen demand.
crops.key_practices: Ridge planting 60×20cm; earthing up at knee-high; tasseling is critical irrigation stage; watch for fall armyworm.

crops.name: Sorghum / Jowar

growing_conditions.soil_type: Black cotton soil (vertisol), red soil, sandy-loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:40:40 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; single superphosphate at sowing
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra, Karnataka, Rajasthan, MP, Telangana, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Sorghum is the most drought-hardy cereal — excels in barren/semi-arid rangeland where other crops fail. Deep root system extracts moisture from 1.5m depth. Ideal dryland crop.
crops.key_practices: 45×15cm spacing; seed treatment with thiram; ratoon crop possible after first harvest; bird scaring at grain fill.

crops.name: Pearl Millet / Bajra

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, sandy-loam, light-textured soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:0 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; responds well to phosphorus
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Haryana, UP, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Most drought-tolerant cereal globally — designed for barren/arid rangeland. Grows where rainfall <400mm. Sandy soils preferred (no waterlogging). Zero water-body affinity.
crops.key_practices: 45×15cm; direct sowing; thinning at 15 DAS; harvest at physiological maturity.

crops.name: Finger Millet / Ragi

growing_conditions.soil_type: Red laterite, sandy-loam, well-drained upland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:40:30 kg/ha; FYM 7.5 t/ha; responds to calcium fertilization
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka (largest), Tamil Nadu, Uttarakhand, AP, Odisha, Maharashtra hill tracts
crops.explanation: Ragi grows in hilly forest margins and red-soil uplands. Tolerates acidic forest soils better than other millets. Nutritionally dense (high calcium). Moderate drought tolerance.
crops.key_practices: Transplanting or direct sowing; 30×10cm spacing; finger branching stage needs moisture; threshing by beating.

crops.name: Barley

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, saline-alkali tolerant soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:20 kg/ha; responds to potash on light soils
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan, UP, MP, Haryana, Punjab, Bihar
crops.explanation: Barley is the most salt-tolerant cereal — grows on marginal barren/alkaline soils where wheat fails. Suited to semi-arid rangeland with low inputs. Short duration (120 days).
crops.key_practices: 23cm row spacing; seed rate 100 kg/ha; 1-2 irrigations at CRI and heading; harvest at golden-yellow stage.

crops.name: Oats

growing_conditions.soil_type: Loamy, clay-loam, tolerates acidic soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:40:20 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha for fodder crops
crops.best_regions_india: Punjab, Haryana, UP, MP, Rajasthan
crops.explanation: Oats thrive in cool climates on cropland and rangeland. Dual-purpose (grain + fodder). Tolerate mildly acidic soils near forest margins. Not water-intensive.
crops.key_practices: 25cm row spacing; 80-100 kg/ha seed rate; 2 cuttings for fodder; grain harvest at hard dough.

crops.name: Rye

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, light-textured, poor fertility soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 50:30:20 kg/ha; minimal input crop
crops.best_regions_india: Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand (hill regions)
crops.explanation: Rye is the hardiest cereal — grows on poor sandy soils and cold hillside rangeland where wheat and barley struggle. Excellent cover crop preventing erosion on slopes.
crops.key_practices: Broadcast sowing; frost tolerant; dual harvest (green fodder + grain); minimal pest issues.

crops.name: Triticale

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, acid soils, marginal lands

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:40:20 kg/ha; similar to wheat management
crops.best_regions_india: Punjab, Haryana, hill states (experimental/emerging crop)
crops.explanation: Triticale (wheat×rye hybrid) combines wheat yield with rye hardiness. Tolerates poor soils, drought, and cold. Emerging crop for marginal lands and rangeland.
crops.key_practices: Wheat-like agronomy; 25cm rows; 100 kg/ha seed rate; dual-purpose grain+fodder.

crops.name: Teff

growing_conditions.soil_type: Vertisol (clay), waterlogged black-cotton soil

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:0 kg/ha; minimal fertilizer input
crops.best_regions_india: Experimental in India; native to Ethiopia; suited to plateau regions
crops.explanation: Teff grows on heavy clay soils that waterlog — unique among cereals. Tolerates poor drainage and semi-arid rangeland. Gluten-free superfood grain gaining global attention.
crops.key_practices: Broadcast sowing (tiny seeds); no-till compatible; harvest in 90-120 days; lodging is main challenge.

crops.name: Foxtail Millet / Kangni

growing_conditions.soil_type: Light sandy to loamy, well-drained upland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:0 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; low input crop
crops.best_regions_india: Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttarakhand
crops.explanation: Foxtail millet thrives on barren/semi-arid rangeland with minimal water. Shortest duration millet (60-75 days). Ideal intercrop. Climate-resilient nutri-cereal.
crops.key_practices: 25×10cm spacing; direct sowing; single weeding at 20 DAS; harvest at straw-yellow stage.

crops.name: Kodo Millet

growing_conditions.soil_type: Gravelly, laterite, shallow soils; forest clearings

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:20 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; organic farming friendly
crops.best_regions_india: MP, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Jharkhand (tribal areas)
crops.explanation: Kodo millet grows on forest margins and tribal uplands with poor gravelly soils. Tolerates waterlogging better than other millets. Important food security crop in tribal India.
crops.key_practices: Broadcasting 8-10 kg/ha; self-sowing capability; ratooning possible; traditional hand-harvesting.

crops.name: Little Millet / Kutki

growing_conditions.soil_type: Red, laterite, shallow upland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:0 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; zero-input possible
crops.best_regions_india: MP, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Jharkhand, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Little millet excels on forest-edge upland soils. Short duration (70-80 days). Grows under partial shade (forest=14%). Traditional rain-fed tribal crop needing zero pesticides.
crops.key_practices: Broadcast/line sowing; minimal management; bird watching at maturity; store as panicle heads.

crops.name: Proso Millet

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, light, well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:0 kg/ha; very low input
crops.best_regions_india: MP, Chhattisgarh, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Proso millet is the fastest-maturing cereal (60 days). Extreme drought tolerance — thrives on barren and rangeland. Zero water requirement from water bodies. Emergency food crop.
crops.key_practices: Direct sowing; 25×10cm spacing; harvest at 60-65 DAS; excellent for intercropping.

crops.name: Chickpea / Chana

growing_conditions.soil_type: Black cotton, sandy-loam, well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:60:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium inoculation; PSB biofertilizer; sulphur 20 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: MP (largest), Rajasthan, Maharashtra, UP, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh
crops.explanation: Chickpea fixes atmospheric nitrogen via Rhizobium — enriches soil. Grows on residual moisture in semi-arid rangeland. Prefers existing cropland with dryland management. No waterlogging tolerance.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm spacing; seed treatment with Rhizobium + Trichoderma; 1-2 irrigations; wilt resistance varieties essential.

crops.name: Pigeon Pea / Tur

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep loamy, red, black soils; tolerates light soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:50:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium seed treatment; DAP placement
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra, Karnataka, MP, UP, Gujarat, AP, Telangana
crops.explanation: Pigeon pea is a perennial nitrogen-fixer with deep taproot (2m). Thrives on forest-edge clearings and dry rangeland. Long duration allows intercropping with sorghum/soybean.
crops.key_practices: 60×20cm spacing; intercrop with sorghum (2:1); spray for pod borer (Helicoverpa); harvest when 80% pods brown.

crops.name: Lentil / Masur

growing_conditions.soil_type: Clay-loam, sandy-loam, well-drained fertile soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium + PSB; molybdenum coating
crops.best_regions_india: MP, UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand
crops.explanation: Lentil is a cool-season pulse grown on residual moisture after monsoon. Needs well-drained cropland. Moderate rangeland tolerance. Cannot survive waterlogging or forest shade.
crops.key_practices: 25cm row spacing; pre-emergence herbicide (pendimethalin); harvest at 90% pod maturity; uprooting preferred over cutting.

crops.name: Green Gram / Moong

growing_conditions.soil_type: Loamy, sandy-loam, well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium; PSB; foliar spray of 2% urea at flowering
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan, Maharashtra, MP, AP, Karnataka, Odisha, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Green gram is the fastest pulse (60-65 days). Fits as catch crop between wheat and rice. Needs warm cropland. Moderate drought tolerance for rangeland edges. Fixes 40-50 kg N/ha.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; summer plowing; 1 irrigation at flowering if rain fails; Cercospora leaf spot management.

crops.name: Black Gram / Urad

growing_conditions.soil_type: Loamy, clay-loam, heavy black soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium; phosphorus critical; FYM 5 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: MP, UP, Rajasthan, AP, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Black gram tolerates heavier soils than moong. Grows on cropland and forest-edge clearings. Short duration (80-90 days). Important dal crop. Moderate shade tolerance (forest=8%).
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; seed rate 15-20 kg/ha; weed control at 20-30 DAS critical; MYMV resistant varieties essential.

crops.name: Kidney Bean / Rajma

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained loamy, hilly alluvial soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Rabi in plains; Kharif in hills (Jun–Oct)

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:60:30 kg/ha; Rhizobium; FYM 10 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: J&K, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Maharashtra hills, Karnataka hills
crops.explanation: Rajma is a cool-climate pulse suited to hilly forest clearings and mountain cropland. Tolerates partial shade and acidic forest soils. Cannot tolerate heat or waterlogging.
crops.key_practices: 45×10cm spacing; stake/trellis for climbers; 4-5 irrigations; harvest when pods dry on plant.

crops.name: Cowpea / Lobia

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, sandy-loam, light dryland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:0 kg/ha; Rhizobium; minimal inputs; drought-hardy
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, MP, AP, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Cowpea is the most drought-tolerant pulse — thrives on barren/rangeland where other pulses fail. Deep root system. Dual-purpose (grain+fodder). Important in arid-zone farming.
crops.key_practices: 45×15cm; direct sowing; intercrop with pearl millet; cowpea mosaic virus management.

crops.name: Field Pea

growing_conditions.soil_type: Clay-loam, well-drained fertile soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:60:30 kg/ha; Rhizobium; phosphorus critical
crops.best_regions_india: UP, MP, Bihar, Rajasthan, Maharashtra, hill states
crops.explanation: Field pea needs cool winters on existing cropland. Moderate rangeland tolerance. Nitrogen-fixing cover crop. Cannot handle heat, drought, or forest shade well.
crops.key_practices: 25cm rows; seed rate 80-100 kg/ha; staking for tall varieties; powdery mildew management.

crops.name: Soybean

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained clay-loam, black cotton soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:80:40 kg/ha; Rhizobium + PSB; sulphur 20 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: MP (54%), Maharashtra, Rajasthan, Karnataka, Telangana
crops.explanation: Soybean is India's #1 oilseed pulse — needs well-managed cropland with assured rainfall. Fixes 80-100 kg N/ha. Forest-edge friendly (shade-tolerant seedling). Not for barren/arid land.
crops.key_practices: 45×5cm; seed rate 70 kg/ha; inoculant essential; Girdle beetle and semilooper management; harvest at R8 stage.

crops.name: Moth Bean

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy desert soils, dunes, extremely light soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 15:30:0 kg/ha; near-zero input; Rhizobium optional
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (Thar Desert), Gujarat arid zone
crops.explanation: Moth bean is the ultimate desert crop — grows on sand dunes and barren land with <250mm rain. Highest heat/drought tolerance among pulses. Zero forest/water affinity.
crops.key_practices: 45×10cm; broadcast sowing; no irrigation needed; harvest in 60-75 days; sand dune stabilizer.

crops.name: Horse Gram / Kulthi

growing_conditions.soil_type: Red gravelly, laterite, poor rocky hillside soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:0 kg/ha; FYM 2 t/ha; zero-input tolerant
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, AP, Chhattisgarh, Uttarakhand, Maharashtra hills
crops.explanation: Horse gram grows on the poorest rocky soils of hillsides and forest margins. Drought and heat resistant. Medicinal pulse (anti-urolithiatic). Can grow on barren slopes.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; broadcast common; zero tillage possible; harvest when pods shatter; traditional crop.

crops.name: Cluster Bean / Guar

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, sandy-loam, arid-zone soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:0 kg/ha; Rhizobium; guar gum is industrial product
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (largest), Gujarat, Haryana, Punjab
crops.explanation: Guar thrives in arid rangeland and barren sandy soils of Thar Desert. Guar gum is India's largest agri-export (industrial demand). No water/forest affinity. Deep taproot.
crops.key_practices: 45×15cm; seed rate 15-20 kg/ha; harvest at physiological maturity; industrial guar vs. vegetable guar varieties.

crops.name: Groundnut / Peanut

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, red sandy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:40 kg/ha; gypsum 500 kg/ha at pegging; Rhizobium; calcium critical
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat (largest), AP, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Groundnut needs light well-drained soils for peg penetration — ideal on sandy cropland and rangeland margins. Gypsum application at pegging is non-negotiable. Not for heavy/waterlogged soils.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; earthing up at 30 DAS; gypsum broadcast at 45 DAS; dig when shell veins darkened.

crops.name: Mustard / Rapeseed

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, loamy, alluvial soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:40:20 kg/ha; sulphur 40 kg/ha (critical for oil quality); borax 10 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (largest), MP, UP, Haryana, Gujarat, WB
crops.explanation: Mustard is India's #1 rabi oilseed. Grows on dryland cropland and semi-arid rangeland. Tolerates frost and dry conditions. Sulphur fertilization is crucial for oil content and quality.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; thinning at 15 DAS; aphid spray at flowering; honeybee colonies for pollination boost 15-20% yield.

crops.name: Sunflower

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep well-drained loamy, black cotton soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:60:30 kg/ha; boron 5 kg/ha; sulphur 25 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka, AP, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, Punjab, Haryana
crops.explanation: Sunflower is photo-insensitive — grows year-round on cropland. Moderate drought tolerance suits rangeland margins. Deep taproot (2m). Needs bee pollination for proper seed set.
crops.key_practices: 60×30cm; seed rate 5-6 kg/ha; bee colonies mandatory (5 hives/ha); head rot management; harvest at ray-floret drooping.

crops.name: Sesame / Til

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, light well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:20 kg/ha; sulphur 20 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: UP (largest), Rajasthan, MP, Gujarat, WB, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Sesame is a dryland oilseed — thrives on semi-arid rangeland and barren edges. Heat tolerant. Cannot tolerate waterlogging. Short duration (80-95 days). Low input, high value.
crops.key_practices: 30×15cm; sowing after first monsoon rain; capsule shattering is harvest indicator; stack and dry inverted.

crops.name: Linseed / Flax

growing_conditions.soil_type: Clay-loam, black cotton, heavy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:20 kg/ha; sulphur 20 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: MP, Chhattisgarh, UP, Maharashtra, Bihar
crops.explanation: Linseed is a cool-season rabi oilseed grown on heavy clay soils. Moderate rangeland tolerance. Dual-purpose (fibre + omega-3 oil). Cannot survive hot or arid conditions.
crops.key_practices: 25cm row spacing; seed rate 25-30 kg/ha; 1-2 irrigations; uprooting at maturity for fibre quality.

crops.name: Castor

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, light dryland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:40:0 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; boron responsive
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat (80%), Rajasthan, AP, Telangana, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Castor is highly drought-tolerant — grows on barren/arid rangeland with deep taproot. Gujarat dominates global production. Industrial oil (non-edible). Tolerates poor, sandy soils.
crops.key_practices: 90×60cm; dibbling; pick ripe spikes every 15-20 days over 6 months; Botrytis gray rot management.

crops.name: Safflower

growing_conditions.soil_type: Black cotton, vertisol, medium to heavy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:30:0 kg/ha; sulphur 20 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (largest), Karnataka, AP, MP, Rajasthan
crops.explanation: Safflower grows on barren dryland and semi-arid rangeland. Extremely drought-tolerant (taproot reaches 3m). Thorny variety resists animal grazing. Used for oil and dye.
crops.key_practices: 45×20cm; dryland sowing on residual moisture; spineless varieties for easy harvest; aphid management.

crops.name: Niger / Ramtil

growing_conditions.soil_type: Red laterite, gravelly, shallow upland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 30:20:10 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; near-zero input
crops.best_regions_india: MP, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Jharkhand, Maharashtra, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Niger grows on forest-edge uplands with poor laterite soils — important tribal oilseed. Shade-tolerant (forest=15%). Bee-pollinated. Organic-friendly zero-input crop.
crops.key_practices: 30×10cm; broadcast common; bee pollination critical; harvest when heads droop; hand threshing.

crops.name: Palm Oil

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep laterite, alluvial, coastal sandy-loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 800g:400g:1200g per palm/year; MgSO₄ 500g; borax 100g
crops.best_regions_india: AP, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Goa, Mizoram, Andaman
crops.explanation: Oil palm has highest oil yield per hectare (4-5 t oil/ha/year). Thrives in tropical forest zones with high rainfall. Agriculture + forest land ideal. Cannot survive dry/barren areas.
crops.key_practices: 9×9m triangular; 143 palms/ha; harvesting Fresh Fruit Bunches every 10-15 days; chisel for harvesting; processing within 24 hours.

crops.name: Coconut

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, coastal alluvial, laterite, red loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500g:320g:1200g per palm/year; organic manure 25 kg/palm; borax 50g
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, AP, Goa, Maharashtra, WB, Odisha, Lakshadweep
crops.explanation: Coconut is the quintessential coastal crop — thrives near water bodies and in forest-mixed plantations. Tolerates urban settings (backyard planting). Salt-spray tolerant. 100+ products from one tree.
crops.key_practices: 7.5×7.5m spacing; basin irrigation; intercropping with cocoa/banana/fodder; coconut rhinoceros beetle management.

crops.name: Cotton (American Upland)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Black cotton vertisol, deep alluvial, medium-heavy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; MgSO₄ 25 kg/ha; foliar KNO₃ at boll development
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat, Maharashtra, Telangana, AP, Haryana, Punjab, Rajasthan, MP
crops.explanation: Cotton needs warm black-cotton soils with deep cracking for root aeration. Grown on existing cropland with Bt technology. Moderate rangeland tolerance. Cannot survive waterlogging or forest shade.
crops.key_practices: 90×60cm; Bt hybrids; bollworm resistant refugia planting mandatory; 5-6 irrigations; 3-4 manual pickings.

crops.name: Cotton (Desi)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Light to medium soils, sandy-loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:20:20 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha; low input
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat, Maharashtra, MP, Rajasthan (rainfed areas)
crops.explanation: Desi cotton is drought-hardier than Bt upland — suited for rainfed rangeland areas. Lower yield but organically viable. Naturally pest-resistant. Short staple fibre.
crops.key_practices: 60×30cm; rainfed management; organic certification possible; hand picking; ginning.

crops.name: Jute

growing_conditions.soil_type: Alluvial, loamy, riverine plains with good moisture

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:30 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; lime 2 t/ha in acid soils
crops.best_regions_india: West Bengal (75%), Bihar, Assam, Odisha, Meghalaya, Tripura
crops.explanation: Jute needs warm waterlogged conditions — high water land is ideal. Grows in Gangetic flood plains. Retting (fibre extraction) requires water bodies. Cannot survive dry/barren areas.
crops.key_practices: Broadcast sowing; thinning at 20 DAS; harvest at 50% flowering; retting in slow-flowing water 15-20 days; ribbon retting saves water.

crops.name: Hemp

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained loamy, alluvial, organic-rich soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 kg/ha; heavy nitrogen feeder; FYM 15 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, J&K (licensed cultivation only)
crops.explanation: Industrial hemp grows in cool hilly forest zones. Shade-tolerant (forest=15%). Deep root system improves soil. Requires government license in India. Fibre and seed dual-purpose.
crops.key_practices: 20×5cm for fibre; 60×30cm for seed; harvest at early seed set for fibre quality; dew retting.

crops.name: Kenaf

growing_conditions.soil_type: Loamy, sandy-loam, moist fertile soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:30 kg/ha; FYM 5 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: AP, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, WB, Bihar
crops.explanation: Kenaf is a jute substitute for drier areas. Moderate water needs (unlike jute). Grows on cropland and forest clearings. Short duration (130-150 days). Biodegradable fibre.
crops.key_practices: 30×5cm for fibre; harvest at early fruiting; stem retting; paper pulp and geotextile applications.

crops.name: Sugarcane

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep alluvial loam, clay-loam, well-drained fertile soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Planted Feb–Mar or Oct; 12-18 month crop

crops.fertilizers: NPK 250:60:60 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; pressmud 10 t/ha; micronutrients (Fe, Zn, Mn)
crops.best_regions_india: UP (largest), Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, AP, Punjab, Haryana, Bihar
crops.explanation: Sugarcane is India's highest-value field crop — needs prime agricultural land with irrigation/high rainfall. Heavy feeder. Not suitable for barren/dry areas. Some water-body proximity beneficial for irrigation.
crops.key_practices: 90cm row spacing; sett planting 3-eye; earthing up at 60/90/120 DAS; trash mulching; ratoon management up to 3 years.

crops.name: Sugar Beet

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep loamy, well-drained, saline-alkaline tolerant

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; boron 5 kg/ha; sodium responsive
crops.best_regions_india: Punjab, Haryana, UP, Maharashtra (experimental/limited in India)
crops.explanation: Sugar beet is an alternative sugar source for cool semi-arid regions. Salt-tolerant — grows on marginally barren/saline soils where sugarcane fails. Short duration vs. sugarcane.
crops.key_practices: 45×20cm; seed rate 10 kg/ha; 6-7 irrigations; harvesting with beet lifter; topping before processing.

crops.name: Tea

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep well-drained acidic loam, laterite, forest soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; pruned every 3-5 years; plucked year-round

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; zinc and boron critical; maintain pH with sulfur
crops.best_regions_india: Assam, West Bengal (Darjeeling), Tamil Nadu (Nilgiris), Kerala, Karnataka, Tripura, Manipur
crops.explanation: Tea is the ultimate forest crop — needs acidic forest soils with shade trees. High rainfall + misty conditions. forest=65% reflects need for shaded, forested hills. Cannot grow on barren/dry land.
crops.key_practices: 1.2×0.6m spacing; shade trees (Albizia, silver oak) at 1:10 ratio; plucking rounds every 7-10 days; manufactured within 24 hours.

crops.name: Coffee Arabica

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep friable laterite, volcanic, forest humus-rich soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing from year 3; harvested Nov–Feb

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:75:100 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; lime if pH <5.0; zinc and boron
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka (Coorg, Chikmagalur, Hassan), Kerala (Wayanad), Tamil Nadu (Nilgiris, Yercaud)
crops.explanation: Arabica coffee is quintessentially a forest understory crop — needs 40-50% shade from silver oak/dadap. forest=72% reflects this strong shade requirement. Quality degrades without forest canopy. High-altitude (1000-1500m).
crops.key_practices: 2×2m spacing; two-tier shading; pulping within 24 hrs of harvest; fermentation 24-36 hrs; ABC (Arabica Berry-Cherry) processing.

crops.name: Coffee Robusta

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep laterite, well-drained tropical forest soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing from year 3; harvested Jan–Mar

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:75:120 kg/ha; organic manure 10 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Karnataka (low elevations), Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Robusta coffee grows at lower altitudes than Arabica but still needs forest shade (forest=68%). More pest-resistant and higher-yielding. Needs warm humid tropical forest zones.
crops.key_practices: 3×3m spacing; single-stem pruning; shade regulation 50%; white stem borer management; instant coffee processing.

crops.name: Rubber

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep laterite, alluvial, well-drained acidic soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial (25-30 year economic life); tapping year-round

crops.fertilizers: NPK 30:30:30 per tree/year; ground cover management; shade management in immature period
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala (90%), Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Tripura, Assam, Meghalaya, Goa
crops.explanation: Rubber plantations function as managed forests — forest=70% reflects canopy requirement. Needs high tropical rainfall. Cannot survive dry/barren environments. 8-year immature period before tapping.
crops.key_practices: 5×5m spacing; 400 trees/ha; tapping at 45cm girth (6-8 years); D2 tapping system; latex collection and coagulation; intercropping with pineapple/banana in immature phase.

crops.name: Tobacco

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam (FCV), black cotton (bidi), alluvial (hookah)

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:100 kg/ha (FCV); potash critical for leaf quality; no chloride fertilizers
crops.best_regions_india: AP & Karnataka (FCV), Gujarat (bidi), UP & Bihar (hookah/chewing)
crops.explanation: Tobacco is soil-type specific: FCV needs sandy-loam, bidi tobacco needs black cotton soil. Moderate rangeland tolerance. High economic value per hectare. Regulated crop.
crops.key_practices: 90×60cm; nursery transplanting; topping and desuckering; barn/sun curing type-specific; grading critical.

crops.name: Cocoa

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep alluvial, volcanic, organic-rich forest soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing from year 3; pods year-round

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:40:100 per tree/year; organic manure 10 kg/tree; FYM important
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, AP (as intercrop under coconut/areca)
crops.explanation: Cocoa is the most shade-dependent crop — forest=75% is highest. Cannot survive in open sun. Grown under coconut/areca canopy. Zero barren/dry tolerance. Needs high consistent moisture.
crops.key_practices: 3×3m under coconut; pod harvesting with sickle; fermentation 5-7 days; sun-drying to 7% moisture; intercrop revenue model.

crops.name: Cashew

growing_conditions.soil_type: Laterite, red sandy-loam, coastal sandy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500g:125g:125g per tree/year; FYM 20 kg/tree; cashew apple composting
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Konkan), Goa, Kerala, Karnataka, AP, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, WB
crops.explanation: Cashew thrives on laterite hillsides and coastal degraded forest land. Tolerates poor barren soils. Excellent for wasteland reclamation. Salt-spray tolerant. Does not need rich agricultural soil.
crops.key_practices: 8×8m spacing; 156 trees/ha; formative pruning first 3 years; tea mosquito bug management; sun drying raw cashew nuts; processing within 6 months.

crops.name: Black Pepper

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep forest humus-rich laterite, volcanic soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial vine; planted Jun; bearing year 3; harvest Jan–Mar

crops.fertilizers: NPK 50g:50g:150g per vine/year; FYM 10 kg/vine; neem cake 1 kg/vine
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala (70%), Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Goa
crops.explanation: Black pepper is forest-climber crop — needs support trees (silver oak, erythrina). forest=70% is essential. Cannot survive open sun, barren, or dry conditions. Monsoon rainfall critical.
crops.key_practices: Trailed on standards at 2.5×2.5m; mulching with leaves; Phytophthora foot rot management; spray Bordeaux mixture; sun drying for 5-7 days.

crops.name: Cardamom (Large)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep forest loam, humus-rich, north-facing slopes

crops.fertilizers: NPK 75:75:150 per plant/year; FYM 5 kg/plant; vermicompost
crops.best_regions_india: Sikkim (70%), Darjeeling, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland
crops.explanation: Large cardamom is the most forest-dependent spice — needs 70-80% shade under Alnus/Himalayan alder. Grows at 600-2000m in Eastern Himalayan moist forests. Zero barren/dry tolerance.
crops.key_practices: 1.5×1.5m; Alnus shade regulation; traditional bhatti curing; chirke and foorkey virus management; ICIMOD improved dryer.

crops.name: Cardamom (Small/Green)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Forest laterite, humus-rich, well-drained slopes

crops.fertilizers: NPK 75:75:150 per plant/year; FYM 5 kg; neem cake 2 kg/plant
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala (Idukki), Karnataka (Coorg), Tamil Nadu (Nilgiris, Anamalais)
crops.explanation: Small cardamom grows in Western Ghats evergreen forest understory at 800-1500m elevation. forest=75%. 'Queen of Spices.' Needs shade regulation to 50-60%. Cannot survive open/barren conditions.
crops.key_practices: 2×2m; shade regulation by lopping; thrips and capsule borer management; flue-pipe curing for green colour retention; graded and packed.

crops.name: Turmeric

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained alluvial loam, red loam, sandy-loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:50:120 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; neem cake 2 t/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Telangana (Nizamabad), AP, Tamil Nadu, Odisha, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Assam
crops.explanation: Turmeric tolerates partial shade — grown as intercrop under coconut/arecanut in forest-garden systems (forest=30%). High water requirement. Rich organic soils needed. Curcumin content is quality measure.
crops.key_practices: 30×15cm raised beds; seed rhizome treatment with mancozeb; mulching with green leaves (15 t/ha); earthing up at 30/60 DAS; boiling, drying, polishing.

crops.name: Ginger

growing_conditions.soil_type: Rich loamy, forest-garden soils, well-drained laterite

crops.fertilizers: NPK 75:50:50 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; neem cake 2 t/ha; Trichoderma for soft rot
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Karnataka, Meghalaya, Assam, Odisha, WB, Himachal Pradesh
crops.explanation: Ginger thrives in forest-garden homesteads (forest=35%). Shade-tolerant — needs 30-40% filtered shade. High organic-matter soils critical. Soft rot (Pythium) is main challenge in humid conditions.
crops.key_practices: 25×20cm raised beds; seed rhizome treatment with Trichoderma + mancozeb; heavy mulching; shade regulation; steam distillation for ginger oil.

crops.name: Chili / Capsicum

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, black cotton, red loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; calcium and boron; micronutrients foliar spray
crops.best_regions_india: AP/Telangana (Guntur), Karnataka, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, MP, Rajasthan
crops.explanation: Chili is India's most important spice economically — grown on prime cropland. Tolerates urban/peri-urban growing (kitchen gardens). Moderate barren/rangeland tolerance for hardy varieties. High capsaicin varieties command premium.
crops.key_practices: 60×45cm transplanting; raised beds; drip irrigation with fertigation; thrips and fruit borer IPM; sun drying to 10% moisture; Teja/Byadgi/Kashmir varieties.

crops.name: Coriander / Dhania

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained loamy, sandy-loam, black soil

crops.fertilizers: NPK 40:30:20 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; sulphur for oil content
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (largest), Gujarat, MP, AP, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Coriander grows well on existing cropland and semi-arid rangeland with cool winters. Drought-tolerant short-duration spice. Dual-purpose: green leaves (cilantro) and dry seed. Essential oil valuable.
crops.key_practices: 25cm rows; seed rate 15-20 kg/ha; split seeds before sowing for better germination; wilt/powdery mildew management; harvest at 90% seed maturity.

crops.name: Cumin / Jeera

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, light well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 30:20:0 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; sulphur 20 kg/ha
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat (largest), Rajasthan (Jodhpur, Barmer), Kutch
crops.explanation: Cumin thrives in arid/semi-arid rangeland and barren dry zones. Grows where rainfall <400mm. Sandy alkaline soils preferred. India produces 70% of world cumin. Highly drought-adapted.
crops.key_practices: 25cm rows; broadcast 12 kg/ha; 3-4 light irrigations; wilt and blight management; harvest when seeds turn brown; threshing by beating.

crops.name: Fenugreek / Methi

growing_conditions.soil_type: Loamy, sandy-loam, clay-loam soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 20:40:20 kg/ha; Rhizobium inoculation; nitrogen-fixer
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (largest), Gujarat, MP, UP, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Fenugreek is a legume-spice — fixes nitrogen. Grows on dryland cropland & rangeland margins. Cold-tolerant. Dual-purpose (seed + fresh leaves). Drought-hardy for semi-arid zones.
crops.key_practices: 25cm rows; soaking seed 12 hours; 2-3 leaf cuttings for green methi; seed harvest at full maturity; Cercospora management.

crops.name: Clove

growing_conditions.soil_type: Rich laterite, volcanic, deep forest humus soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 300g:250g:750g per tree/year; FYM 25 kg/tree; neem cake 2 kg
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka (limited; experimental in NE India)
crops.explanation: Clove is an evergreen forest tree needing consistent humidity and forest canopy proximity. forest=75%. Zero barren tolerance. Extremely sensitive to dry/hot winds. High-value low-volume spice.
crops.key_practices: 6×6m spacing; shade management; hand-picking unopened buds; sun-drying 4-5 days; oil distillation residues.

crops.name: Nutmeg

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep laterite, volcanic, well-drained forest soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing year 7; harvest Jun–Aug peak

crops.fertilizers: NPK 250g:250g:500g per tree/year; FYM 20 kg/tree
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala (Ernakulam, Thrissur), Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Goa
crops.explanation: Nutmeg is a tropical forest tree providing dual products (nutmeg seed + mace). Needs forest-like environment (forest=72%). Dioecious — needs male:female ratio 1:10. Cannot survive dry/barren conditions.
crops.key_practices: 8×8m spacing; sex identification at year 4; hand harvesting split fruits; mace separation and sun-drying separately; essential oil distillation.

crops.name: Vanilla

growing_conditions.soil_type: Rich humus, well-drained laterite, forest garden soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial vine; bearing year 3; manual pollination Feb–Apr

crops.fertilizers: Organic-only: FYM 10 kg/vine; vermicompost; mulching with forest leaf litter
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Goa (limited high-value cultivation)
crops.explanation: Vanilla is the world's most labor-intensive crop — hand-pollination is mandatory (no natural pollinators in India). Needs forest shade support trees. forest=75%. Highest-value spice per kg.
crops.key_practices: 2.5m spacing on support trees (gliricidia/erythrina); hand pollination each morning; beans harvested at 6-8 months green; curing process 3-6 months (killing, sweating, drying, conditioning).

crops.name: Saffron

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained karewa (lacustrine), sandy-loam, alkaline soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Planted Aug; flowering Oct–Nov; 6-week harvest window

crops.fertilizers: NPK 90:60:45 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; zero-chemical preferred
crops.best_regions_india: J&K (Pampore, Pulwama — only GI-tagged region in India)
crops.explanation: Saffron grows exclusively in cold high-altitude (1500-2500m) semi-arid rangeland with dry summers and cold winters. Pampore karewa soil is unique globally. Barren tolerance=35% reflects stony/arid conditions. World's costliest spice.
crops.key_practices: 20×10cm; corm planting 15cm deep; hand harvesting flowers at dawn; 3 stigmas per flower; drying at 50°C; 150,000 flowers = 1 kg dry saffron.

crops.name: Fennel / Saunf

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, loamy, saline tolerant

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:30 kg/ha; FYM 15 t/ha; Azotobacter + PSB
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat (largest), Rajasthan, UP, MP, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Fennel grows on semi-arid cropland and rangeland margins in cool winters. Moderate drought tolerance. Gujarat dominates production. Volatile oil content determines grade quality.
crops.key_practices: 45×20cm transplanting; 5-6 irrigations; aphid management critical; harvest at grey-green umbel stage; shade-drying preserves oil content.

crops.name: Potato

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, alluvial, friable soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Rabi (Oct–Feb in plains); Kharif in hills

crops.fertilizers: NPK 150:60:100 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; calcium and sulphur; potassium is key for tuber quality
crops.best_regions_india: UP (largest), WB, Bihar, Gujarat, MP, Punjab, HP, J&K
crops.explanation: Potato is India's most important vegetable — grows on prime cropland with irrigation. Urban peri-urban friendly (urban=5%). High input-high output crop. Cool season. Cold storage essential.
crops.key_practices: 60×20cm on ridges; seed tuber treatment; earthing up at 25/45 DAS; late blight management (metalaxyl+mancozeb); dehaulming 10 days before harvest.

crops.name: Onion

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, alluvial, red loam

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; sulphur 45 kg/ha (critical for pungency); micronutrients
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Nashik), Karnataka, MP, Gujarat, Bihar, Rajasthan, AP
crops.explanation: Onion is India's #2 vegetable by production. Needs existing cropland with good drainage. Moderate barren/rangeland tolerance for kharif crop. Sulphur fertilization directly affects pungency and storage life.
crops.key_practices: 15×10cm transplanting; raised flat beds; drip irrigation; neck rot management; curing in field 3-5 days; cold storage at 0-2°C.

crops.name: Tomato

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, loamy, red soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Year-round with protected cultivation; Rabi in plains

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; calcium (prevents blossom-end rot); boron
crops.best_regions_india: AP, Karnataka, MP, Maharashtra, Odisha, WB, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Tomato is highly adaptable to urban/peri-urban farming (urban=5%). Greenhouse-friendly. Needs well-managed cropland. Not drought-tolerant. High economic returns per hectare. Staking improves quality.
crops.key_practices: 60×45cm staked; pruning single/double stem; drip fertigation; tuta absoluta and leaf curl virus management; ethylene ripening in controlled atmosphere.

crops.name: Brinjal / Eggplant

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained fertile loamy, sandy-loam, alluvial
growing_conditions.growing_season: Year-round in tropics; Kharif/Rabi in subtropics

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; calcium and boron critical
crops.best_regions_india: WB, Odisha, Bihar, Gujarat, AP, Karnataka, Maharashtra, MP
crops.explanation: Brinjal is India's most widely grown vegetable. Urban/peri-urban friendly (backyard gardens). Tolerates diverse soils. Moderate heat and drought tolerance. Shoot and fruit borer is #1 pest.
crops.key_practices: 60×60cm; nursery raising 4-5 weeks; pheromone traps for fruit borer; drip irrigation; harvest every 5-7 days at glossy stage.

crops.name: Cabbage

growing_conditions.soil_type: Clay-loam, well-drained fertile soils rich in organic matter
growing_conditions.growing_season: Rabi (Sep–Feb in plains); year-round in hills

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; boron and molybdenum essential
crops.best_regions_india: WB, UP, Odisha, Bihar, MP, Gujarat, HP, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Cabbage needs cool weather and fertile organic-rich cropland. Can be grown in urban settings (kitchen gardens). Not drought-tolerant. Diamond-back moth is most serious pest worldwide.
crops.key_practices: 45×45cm transplanting; 30-day nursery; Bt spray for DBM; head splitting indicates over-maturity; harvest heads with 2-3 wrapper leaves.

crops.name: Cauliflower

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained loamy rich in organic matter
growing_conditions.growing_season: Rabi (Sep–Feb in plains); Kharif in hills

crops.fertilizers: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha; FYM 25 t/ha; boron 10 kg/ha (prevents browning); molybdenum (prevents whiptail)
crops.best_regions_india: WB, Bihar, UP, Odisha, Punjab, Haryana, MP, HP
crops.explanation: Cauliflower is more temperature-sensitive than cabbage. Boron deficiency causes brown/hollow curds. Needs prime cropland. Blanching (leaf tying) maintains white curd. Urban-friendly.
crops.key_practices: 45×45cm; blanching by leaf tying at curd initiation; harvest compact white curds; early-mid-late varieties for staggered production.

crops.name: Garlic

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, loamy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; sulphur 40 kg/ha (critical for allicin/flavour)
crops.best_regions_india: MP (largest), Gujarat, Rajasthan, UP, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Garlic needs cool short days for bulbing. Grows on dryland cropland and semi-arid rangeland margins. Sulphur-responding crop — determines pungency and medicinal value. Moderate drought tolerance.
crops.key_practices: 15×10cm clove planting; mulching conserves moisture; no overhead irrigation (purple blotch risk); curing 7-10 days; cold storage at 0-2°C.

crops.name: Okra / Lady's Finger

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, loamy, well-drained soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:30:30 kg/ha; FYM 15 t/ha; zinc and boron
crops.best_regions_india: WB, Bihar, AP, Odisha, Gujarat, Maharashtra, UP, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Okra is a warm-season vegetable tolerating heat and moderate drought. Grows on cropland and rangeland margins. Fast-growing — first harvest at 45 DAS. Yellow vein mosaic virus is main challenge.
crops.key_practices: 45×30cm; seed treatment with imidacloprid for YVMV; picking every 2-3 days; smooth tender fruits at 5-7cm preferred; avoid overmature fibrous pods.

crops.name: Pumpkin / Kaddu

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, alluvial, fertility-rich soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 50:25:25 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; potassium for fruit quality
crops.best_regions_india: UP, MP, Bihar, WB, Odisha, Maharashtra, Assam, Karnataka
crops.explanation: Pumpkin is a spreading vine crop needing open ground on cropland/rangeland. Moderate forest-edge tolerance (can climb trees). Stores 3-6 months without refrigeration. Excellent in intercropping.
crops.key_practices: 3×2m pit sowing; training vines; pollination assistance improves fruit set; harvest when stem dries and fruit sounds hollow; maturity marked by skin hardening.

crops.name: Cucumber

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, fertile, organic-rich soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 50:25:25 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; calcium for crispness
crops.best_regions_india: UP, MP, Haryana, Punjab, Bihar, Karnataka, AP, WB
crops.explanation: Cucumber is a rapid-maturing vine crop ideal for cropland and peri-urban polyhouse cultivation (urban=4%). High water requirement. Cannot tolerate frost or barren soils. Parthenocarpic varieties for greenhouses.
crops.key_practices: 1.5×0.5m; trellis/staking for quality; harvest every 2-3 days; downy/powdery mildew management; gynoecious F1 hybrids dominate; protected cultivation growing rapidly.

crops.name: Cassava / Tapioca

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, laterite, red soils, poor/marginal soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:100 kg/ha; FYM 12.5 t/ha; potassium critical for starch
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Tamil Nadu, AP, Karnataka, Nagaland, Meghalaya
crops.explanation: Cassava is the ultimate food-security crop — grows on poor soils (barren=25%, rangeland=30%) and forest clearings (forest=20%) where cereals fail. Drought-resilient after establishment. Starch + ethanol dual use.
crops.key_practices: Stem cuttings 20cm; 90×90cm spacing; earthing up; mosaic virus management; harvest before rainy season; processing within 72 hours (cyanide content).

crops.name: Sweet Potato

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, light soils, well-drained

crops.fertilizers: NPK 75:50:75 kg/ha; FYM 10 t/ha; potassium for tuber quality
crops.best_regions_india: Odisha, UP, WB, Bihar, Maharashtra, MP, Karnataka, Jharkhand
crops.explanation: Sweet potato grows on marginal soils (barren+rangeland) where other vegetables struggle. Drought-tolerant. High beta-carotene (orange-fleshed varieties). Vine cuttings = zero seed cost. Quick food-security crop.
crops.key_practices: Ridge planting; 60×30cm vine cuttings; earthing up; weevil management; harvest at 120 DAS; curing for storage (30°C, 85% RH for 5 days).

crops.name: Yam

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep loamy, forest garden soils, well-drained fertile

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:60:80 kg/ha; FYM 15 t/ha; potassium critical
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala, Tamil Nadu, AP, Odisha, WB, NE India, Bihar
crops.explanation: Yam is a forest-garden crop — needs deep organic soils under partial shade (forest=35%). Climbing vine needs staking. Important food in tribal and coastal areas. Large tubers (5-20 kg).
crops.key_practices: Tuber piece planting; staking with bamboo poles; mulching; harvest when leaves yellow; traditional pit storage; processed into chips/flour.

crops.name: Taro / Colocasia

growing_conditions.soil_type: Marshy, waterlogged, alluvial, organic-rich wetland soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:50:100 kg/ha; FYM 15 t/ha; potassium important
crops.best_regions_india: WB, Bihar, UP, Assam, Kerala, Jharkhand, Odisha, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Taro is a semi-aquatic crop — water=25% is highest among vegetables. Thrives in marshy wetlands and forest streams. Shade-tolerant (forest=30%). Cannot survive barren/dry conditions. Important in wetland farming systems.
crops.key_practices: 60×45cm; standing water culture or upland culture; corm planting; blight management; harvest when leaves yellow; shade-drying corms; oxalate content reduced by boiling.

crops.name: Spinach

growing_conditions.soil_type: Rich loamy, sandy-loam, organic-rich fertile soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 80:30:20 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; nitrogen for leaf yield; iron-rich variety selection
crops.best_regions_india: All India during winter; year-round in hills
crops.explanation: Spinach is the most urban-friendly vegetable (urban=5%) — grows in small spaces, containers, balconies. Rapid cycle (30 days to first harvest). Cool-season. High iron and calcium content. Multiple harvests.
crops.key_practices: Broadcast or 15cm rows; successive sowing every 15 days for continuous supply; 4-6 cuttings possible; aphid management; baby spinach premium market.

crops.name: Carrot

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep sandy-loam, loose friable soils free of stones

crops.fertilizers: NPK 60:40:60 kg/ha; FYM 20 t/ha; potassium for root quality; no fresh manure (causes forking)
crops.best_regions_india: HP, J&K, Karnataka, AP, Tamil Nadu, Punjab, Haryana, UP
crops.explanation: Carrot needs deep loose soils — stones cause forking. Cool-season root vegetable. Good for cropland and urban container gardening. Beta-carotene content is health USP. Cannot survive waterlogging.
crops.key_practices: Raised flat beds; 25×5cm thinned; no fresh FYM (apply 3 months before); irrigation every 7-10 days; harvest at 90 DAS for tender roots; cold storage at 0°C.

crops.name: Mango

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep alluvial, laterite, sandy-loam; tolerates poor soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 1000g:500g:1000g per tree/year (mature); FYM 50 kg/tree; micronutrients foliar spray
crops.best_regions_india: UP (largest), AP, Karnataka, Bihar, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, WB, Kerala
crops.explanation: Mango is India's national fruit — grows on diverse soils from cropland to barren hillsides and rangeland. Urban-friendly (compound planting). Alternate bearing management is key challenge. 500+ varieties in India.
crops.key_practices: 10×10m; 100 trees/ha (high density 5×5m: 400/ha); paclobutrazol for regular bearing; fruit fly management; hot water treatment 48°C for 5 min post-harvest; carbide ripening banned.

crops.name: Banana

growing_conditions.soil_type: Rich deep alluvial, volcanic, well-drained loamy soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 200:50:300 per plant/crop; FYM 10 kg/plant; Azospirillum + PSB biofertilizers
crops.best_regions_india: Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Gujarat, AP, Karnataka, Bihar, WB, Assam, Kerala
crops.explanation: Banana needs rich organic soils with consistent moisture — ideal on irrigated cropland and forest-garden systems (forest=20%). High potassium demand. Water=5% reflects irrigation needs. Cannot survive drought or barren land.
crops.key_practices: 1.5×1.5m; tissue culture plants; desuckering to single follower; bunch covering with blue polyethylene; propping; Panama wilt (Fusarium TR4) biosecurity.

crops.name: Papaya

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, alluvial, light fertile soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Planted any month; bearing starts 9-12 months; 3-year crop life

crops.fertilizers: NPK 200:200:200 g/plant/year; FYM 20 kg/plant; calcium and boron important
crops.best_regions_india: AP, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, WB, Tamil Nadu, Bihar, MP
crops.explanation: Papaya is the fastest commercial fruit tree — bearing in 9 months. Urban-friendly (urban=5% — compound planting). Moderate forest tolerance (homestead gardens). Cannot survive waterlogging (stem rot). Papain is industrial product.
crops.key_practices: 2×2m; dioecious (keep 10% males); Ring Spot Virus resistant varieties; latex tapping for papain; harvest at colour break stage.

crops.name: Guava

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, alluvial, laterite; extremely soil-tolerant
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; two crops (rainy Jun–Sep; winter Oct–Feb)

crops.fertilizers: NPK 600:300:300 g/tree/year; FYM 25 kg/tree; zinc sulphate foliar spray
crops.best_regions_india: UP (Allahabad), MP, Bihar, Maharashtra, AP, Punjab, Gujarat
crops.explanation: Guava is the most soil-adaptable fruit — grows from acid laterite to alkaline wasteland. Highest urban (6%) — excellent for kitchen gardens and compounds. Tolerates barren/rangeland. Virtually indestructible tree. Wilt (Fusarium/nematode) is the only serious threat.
crops.key_practices: 6×6m; high-density 3×3m meadow orcharding; crop regulation by bahar treatment (water stress + pruning); fruit fly bagging; cold storage at 8-10°C.

crops.name: Pomegranate / Anar

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, gravelly, calcareous, dry soils; salt-tolerant

crops.fertilizers: NPK 625:250:250 g/tree/year; FYM 25 kg/tree; micronutrients foliar
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Solapur, Nashik), Karnataka, AP, Gujarat, Rajasthan, HP
crops.explanation: Pomegranate is the top drought-hardy fruit — thrives on barren rocky hillsides (barren=32%) and dry rangeland (rangeland=35%). Zero water-body need. India's highest-value fruit export. Bacterial blight (Oily Spot) is main threat.
crops.key_practices: 5×4m; 500 trees/ha; bahar regulation by water stress; aril quality determines grade; Bhagwa variety dominates; fruit cracking management; MAPs (Modified Atmosphere Packaging) for export.

crops.name: Grapes

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, deep well-drained, gravelly, calcareous soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; pruning Oct (hot tropics); harvest Jan–Apr

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500:300:500 g/vine/year; FYM 25 kg/vine; calcium nitrate; micronutrients
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Nashik, Sangli, Solapur), Karnataka (Bijapur), AP, Tamil Nadu
crops.explanation: Grapes need dry climate with hot days and cool nights — semi-arid cropland ideal. Moderate barren tolerance (barren=25%). Irrigation via drip is essential. India's highest-value fruit per hectare. Table grapes and raisins dual market.
crops.key_practices: 3×1.8m Y-trellis; 1850 vines/ha; back pruning + forward pruning; GA3 berry sizing; downy mildew management (metalaxyl); export grape protocol (GlobalGAP).

crops.name: Apple

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained deep loam, silt-loam, mountain valley soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial deciduous; flowering Apr; harvest Aug–Oct

crops.fertilizers: NPK 700g:350g:700g per tree/year; FYM 35 kg/tree; calcium chloride spray for bitter pit prevention
crops.best_regions_india: J&K (Shopian, Sopore), HP (Shimla, Kullu, Kinnaur), Uttarakhand, Arunachal Pradesh
crops.explanation: Apple needs winter chilling (1000+ hours below 7°C) — exclusive to mountain regions at 1500-3000m. Forest-friendly (forest=25%) in mixed temperate orchards. Cannot grow in plains/barren/dry conditions. India's highest-value temperate fruit.
crops.key_practices: 4×4m; rootstock M9/MM106 for high density; cross-pollination (Golden Delicious as pollinizer); calcium spray for firmness; CA storage (1-2°C, 2% O₂, 1% CO₂).

crops.name: Orange / Citrus

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep well-drained sandy-loam, alluvial, laterite soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; Ambe bahar (Feb flowering); harvest Nov–Jan

crops.fertilizers: NPK 600:200:200 g/tree/year; FYM 25 kg; zinc and manganese foliar; Trichoderma root application
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Nagpur mandarin), AP/Telangana (sweet orange), Punjab/HP (kinnow), NE India (khasi mandarin), Assam
crops.explanation: Citrus grows on diverse soils from cropland to forest margins and rangeland. Moderate urban adaptability. Phytophthora gummosis and Citrus Greening (HLB) are biggest global threats. Bahar treatment regulates cropping season.
crops.key_practices: 6×6m; basin irrigation transitioning to drip; Phytophthora management (metalaxyl trunk paste); psyllid control for HLB; waxing and sizing for export; kinnow juice processing.

crops.name: Lemon / Lime

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, well-drained, tolerates slightly alkaline

crops.fertilizers: NPK 400:200:200 g/tree/year; FYM 20 kg; zinc foliar spray; organic mulching
crops.best_regions_india: AP, Maharashtra, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Assam, Rajasthan
crops.explanation: Lemon/lime is the most urban-adaptable citrus (urban=5%). Grows in pots, compounds, and kitchen gardens. Moderate barren/rangeland tolerance. Year-round demand. Citrus canker is key disease.
crops.key_practices: 5×5m; 400 trees/ha; seedling/budded plants; year-round harvesting; citrus canker management (copper spray); nimbu pani/pickle/oil uses.

crops.name: Sapota / Chiku

growing_conditions.soil_type: Laterite, alluvial, sandy-loam, coastal soils; versatile

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500:250:500 g/tree/year; FYM 40 kg/tree; zinc and boron
crops.best_regions_india: Gujarat (Junagadh/Valsad), Maharashtra, Karnataka, AP, Tamil Nadu, WB
crops.explanation: Sapota is a hardy tropical evergreen — tolerates both coastal and inland conditions. Forest-garden friendly (forest=15%). Urban compound tree (urban=4%). Extended shelf life if harvested mature. Chicle (gum) was original product.
crops.key_practices: 8×8m; 156 trees/ha; minimal pruning; ethylene ripening (100 ppm for 24 hrs); seed weevil management; harvested when skin turns khaki and scratches show green.

crops.name: Pineapple

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy-loam, laterite, acidic, well-drained slopes
growing_conditions.growing_season: Planted Jun; harvest 15-18 months; ratoon for 2-3 cycles

crops.fertilizers: NPK 12:4:12 g/plant/crop; FYM 5 kg/plant; potassium critical for sweetness; foliar urea
crops.best_regions_india: Meghalaya, Assam, Manipur, Tripura, WB, Kerala, Karnataka, Mizoram
crops.explanation: Pineapple thrives on acidic forest slopes in NE India (forest=30%). Shade-tolerant — grown as rubber/coconut plantation intercrop. Acid soil lover (pH 4.5-5.5). Cannot survive alkaline or waterlogged conditions.
crops.key_practices: Double row 30×45×90cm; sucker/crown planting; ethephon flower induction at 12 months; basal rot management; harvest at 1/3 yellow stage; canning and juice processing.

crops.name: Avocado

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained laterite, volcanic, loamy, non-waterlogging

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500:200:500 g/tree/year; FYM 25 kg/tree; zinc and boron foliar
crops.best_regions_india: Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Maharashtra, Sikkim (emerging crop in India)
crops.explanation: Avocado needs tropical/subtropical forest-garden conditions (forest=35%). Shade-tolerant when young. Cannot tolerate waterlogging (Phytophthora root rot). India's luxury fruit market growing rapidly. High healthy-fat content.
crops.key_practices: 8×8m; Hass/Fuerte varieties; grafted plants; mulching critical; Phytophthora management; harvest at maturity (not ripeness); ripening off-tree 5-7 days.

crops.name: Strawberry

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained sandy-loam, organic-rich, raised beds
growing_conditions.growing_season: Planted Sep–Oct; harvest Dec–Mar; annual in India

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:80:80 kg/ha; FYM 40 t/ha; calcium nitrate for firmness; drip fertigation weekly
crops.best_regions_india: Maharashtra (Mahabaleshwar), HP, J&K, Uttarakhand, Meghalaya, Nainital, Karnataka, Punjab
crops.explanation: Strawberry has the highest urban (8%) — grows in small spaces, containers, balconies, vertical farms, and polyhouses. Premium price crop. Needs cool climate and well-managed soil. Most popular agri-tourism crop.
crops.key_practices: Raised beds with plastic mulch; drip+fertigation; 30×30cm; runner plants from tissue culture; Botrytis management; harvest daily at 3/4 red stage; cold chain mandatory (2-4°C, 36-hour shelf life).

crops.name: Olive

growing_conditions.soil_type: Well-drained calcareous, gravelly, sandy-loam soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 500:200:300 g/tree/year; FYM 20 kg; boron and zinc
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (Udaipur/Jodhpur), J&K, HP (experimental; ICAR-CIAH promoted)
crops.explanation: Olive is the ultimate barren-land fruit — barren=38% and rangeland=30%. Thrives on rocky calcareous slopes where nothing else grows. Drought-hardy Mediterranean crop. Zero water needs. India's olive mission is expanding.
crops.key_practices: 6×6m; varieties: Picual, Arbequina, Frantoio; cross-pollination needed; oil extraction within 24 hours of harvest; cold-press extra virgin olive oil.

crops.name: Date Palm

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy desert, saline, alkaline soils; extreme soil tolerance

crops.fertilizers: NPK 1200g:600g:1800g per palm/year; FYM 40 kg; sulphur for pH correction
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan (Jaisalmer, Bikaner, Jodhpur), Gujarat (Kutch), HP (experimental)
crops.explanation: Date palm has highest barren (55%) — purpose-built for desert. Grows in Thar Desert where temperatures hit 50°C. Saline groundwater tolerant. Zero forest affinity. India's date mission promoting Barhee/Medjool varieties.
crops.key_practices: 8×8m; tissue culture offshoot planting; artificial pollination by hand (Dusting male pollen); bunch covering; fumigation for stored pest; dehydration for Chhuhara processing.

crops.name: Jackfruit / Kathal

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep alluvial, laterite, well-drained fertile forest soils

crops.fertilizers: NPK 250:100:250 g/tree/year; FYM 30 kg/tree; minimal management
crops.best_regions_india: Kerala (state fruit), Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, WB, Bihar, Assam, Goa, Tripura
crops.explanation: Jackfruit is a forest-garden tree (forest=40%). World's largest tree-borne fruit (up to 80 kg). Grows naturally in tropical moist deciduous forests. Zero barren tolerance. Kerala declared it state fruit in 2018. Meat substitute emerging market.
crops.key_practices: 10×10m; seedling or grafted; minimal pest issues; harvest when knocking sounds hollow; bulb extraction; green jackfruit as vegetable; dehydrated chips value addition.

crops.name: Litchi

growing_conditions.soil_type: Deep alluvial, fertile, well-drained soils with high organic matter
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing year 5-6; harvest May–Jun (30-day window)

crops.fertilizers: NPK 800:400:600 g/tree/year (mature); FYM 50 kg/tree; zinc and boron
crops.best_regions_india: Bihar (Muzaffarpur — 60%), WB, Jharkhand, Assam, Uttarakhand, Punjab, UP, Tripura
crops.explanation: Litchi needs winter chill for flower induction + hot humid summer for fruit development. Forest-garden friendly (forest=20%). Highly perishable — 48-72 hour shelf life. Controlled atmosphere extends to 30 days.
crops.key_practices: 10×10m; 100 trees/ha; air-layering propagation; fruit cracking management (uniform irrigation); harvest with stalk retention; SO₂ treatment for browning prevention; cold chain essential (2-5°C).

crops.name: Moringa / Drumstick

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy to loamy, well-drained; tolerates poor/marginal soils
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; first harvest 8 months; pods year-round

crops.fertilizers: NPK 100:50:50 g/tree/year; FYM 15 kg/tree; minimal input
crops.best_regions_india: Tamil Nadu, AP, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Gujarat, Odisha, Maharashtra
crops.explanation: Moringa is the 'miracle tree' — grows on barren wasteland (barren=38%) and degraded rangeland (rangeland=40%). Most nutritious plant on earth (gram-for-gram). Drought-hardy, fast-growing, virtually pest-free. Wastewater treatment capability.
crops.key_practices: 3×3m; seed/cutting planting; annual pruning to 1m for leaf harvest; year-round pod picking; leaf drying for superfood powder; seed-based water purification.

crops.name: Jatropha (Biofuel)

growing_conditions.soil_type: Sandy, gravelly, degraded, marginal wastelands; any soil
growing_conditions.growing_season: Perennial; bearing year 2-3; seed harvest Nov–Dec

crops.fertilizers: NPK 30:20:20 g/plant/year; FYM 5 kg; near-zero input; wasteland crop
crops.best_regions_india: Rajasthan, Gujarat, MP, Chhattisgarh, AP, Tamil Nadu (wasteland plantations)
crops.explanation: Jatropha has highest rangeland (50%) — purpose-designed for degraded wasteland reclamation. Non-edible oilseed for biodiesel. Grows on the worst soils globally. Drought-proof. India's National Biofuel Mission crop (Phase-II).
crops.key_practices: 3×2m; live fencing; seed collection 2-3 rounds; oil extraction by mechanical expeller; trans-esterification for biodiesel; seed cake as organic fertilizer.
